# Reaffirmation Agreement - Keep Property in Ch. 7 [2026]
> Should you sign a reaffirmation agreement in Chapter 7? Risks, benefits, and alternatives for keeping your car or house. 2026 guide.

## About
reaffirmationagreement.org is part of the Open Bankruptcy Project (openbankruptcyproject.org), a 501(c)(3) nonprofit building free, open-source tools for bankruptcy court transparency.

## Key Pages
- Reaffirming Your Car Loan in Chapter 7 Bankruptcy: https://reaffirmationagreement.org/car-reaffirmation.html
- Reaffirmation Agreement Deadline - When Must You File?: https://reaffirmationagreement.org/deadline.html
- Reaffirmation Agreement - Keep Property in Ch. 7: https://reaffirmationagreement.org/
- Judge Denied Your Reaffirmation Agreement - What Happens Now?: https://reaffirmationagreement.org/judge-denied.html
- Mortgage Reaffirmation: Do You Need to Reaffirm Your Home Loan?: https://reaffirmationagreement.org/mortgage-reaffirmation.html
- Changed Your Mind? How to Rescind a Reaffirmation Agreement: https://reaffirmationagreement.org/rescission.html
- Ride-Through vs Reaffirmation vs Redemption - Your Options: https://reaffirmationagreement.org/ride-through.html
- The Risks of Reaffirmation: What Your Creditor Won't Tell You: https://reaffirmationagreement.org/risks.html
- When to Say No to Reaffirmation - Protecting Yourself: https://reaffirmationagreement.org/when-to-say-no.html
- Reaffirmation Without an Attorney - Pro Se Requirements: https://reaffirmationagreement.org/without-attorney.html
- Privacy Policy: https://reaffirmationagreement.org/privacy.html

Content is provided for educational purposes. Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (4.9M cases, 94 districts).
